visual_notes: Rose quartz has a characteristic soft, diffuse, milky-pink quality from the light-scattering fibrous inclusions. It is never facet-grade transparent in classic form. The color is gentle and warm — not vivid like ruby or pink tourmaline but consistently soft. Star rose quartz cut as cabochon shows the six-rayed star most vividly under a point light source.
prompt_fragments: rose quartz massive — diffuse pink warmth in translucent quartz fog
star rose quartz cabochon — six-rayed asterism in milky blush
Venus stone: Brazil pegmatite pink dispersed through massive silica
vibe_notes: Rose quartz is mineral warmth — not vivid, not sharp, not crystalline. It diffuses its pink into the whole mass and softens light into rose. It is the texture of a gentle thing, the color of trust and care.
folklore_summary: Rose quartz has one of the most robust modern metaphysical followings of any mineral. Ancient Assyrian and Roman cosmetic recipes include rose quartz facial rollers — a practice that has seen a massive modern revival. Egyptian legend held that rose quartz was a gift from Aphrodite (via Eros) to huma
